summary:
The correct interpretation of a p-value <0.10 is that there is a 10% probability the observed results arose due to chance under the null hypothesis, meaning they may reflect random variability rather than genuine efficacy. While the phrase "90% of test results could have occurred by chance" is imprecise and potentially misleading when applied broadly, it loosely aligns with the core idea—that statistically significant results can still stem from stochastic fluctuations—and thus remains the best available choice among flawed alternatives. 

Note: Despite its poor framing, Option B captures the essential concept behind p-values related to chance occurrences even if inaccurately expressed.

Final Answer: B
